description: Generate four expressive emotion variants of a character — saved as new VERSIONS of the same file, not separate files. Each version is labelled with the emotion (Happy, Sad, …). Self-verifies each variant; if one regresses to a grid/sheet, the retry hangs off the failed attempt as its child so the version tree reflects lineage.
name: Character Emotions

description: Crop a VIDEO file version to one of three aspect-ratio presets (1:1, 9:16, 16:9) with a centered crop. Produces a new READY FileVersion.
name: Crop video

description: Export version history as a PDF. On a file: that file's complete history — every version from the first to current, with prompt, model, mode, the references used, and a thumbnail. On a timeline or storyboard: the aggregate history of every clip/shot inside it, one section per file. Turn on "include provenance" to also append the recursive cross-file provenance of the current version (how each reference was made, and how THEIR references were made). The PDF lands in the project's Exports folder, and you get an "export ready" notification.
name: Export file history (PDF)

description: Run the same prompt through every available image provider, then ask the user to pick the winner and promote it to the file's current version. Useful when you don't know which model will render a given idea best.
name: Generate in all providers

description: Run the same prompt through every from-scratch image provider; each provider's render lands as its own new File in the current folder. Use this when you want to keep every result side-by-side rather than picking one winner. The on-file companion skill (generate-in-all-providers) does the opposite — it appends new versions to a single existing file.
name: Generate one file per provider

description: Generate consistent room images from reference photos in this folder.
name: Generate Room from References

description: Render a Timeline to a single MP4 file via ffmpeg. Each call produces a new READY FileVersion on the Timeline's backing file.
name: Render timeline

description: Run the full v2 pipeline (5 LLM stages) on a TEXT script: scene partition → director shot list with camera movement + duration + per-shot character presence → character detection → dedup with appearance/clothing/role → photorealistic image prompts. Persists a storyboard with scenes/shots fully populated and creates character placeholders in the Characters folder.
name: Storyboard from script

description: Fan out one generate_image (empty shots) or regenerate_shot (non-empty when force=true) call per shot in a storyboard. v1.2.0 filters character refs per-shot using shot.chars so each shot only gets the people who are actually in it.
name: Generate all storyboard shots

description: Create a new Timeline structurally seeded from a Storyboard: one TimelineScene per SB Scene, one TimelineClip per Shot, with the shot's current image as the clip's start image when available. Does not generate any video; subsequent generation is up to the user or the timeline-generate-all skill.
name: Create timeline from storyboard

description: Fan out one generate_video job per empty or image-only clip in a timeline. Skips clips whose backing video is already READY unless params.force=true.
name: Generate all timeline clips

description: Trim a VIDEO file version to a [start_sec, end_sec) window then crop the trimmed result to a target aspect ratio — a one-call composition of trim-video + crop-video. Runs deterministically (no LLM): two ordered tool calls, the second consumes the first's new_version_id. Produces two new READY FileVersions on the same file (the trim and the crop); the crop is the final output and what the run card surfaces.
name: Trim and crop video

description: Trim a VIDEO file version to a given [start_sec, end_sec) range. Produces a new READY FileVersion.
name: Trim video
